title: IdentityRegistry — personhood lifecycle (per nullifier)
scope: one machine instance per person (nullifier = HMAC(OIB, pepper)); the contract multiplexes all of them

states.label: Active (claimed)
states.guard: identities[nullifier].anchor != address(0); anchorToNullifier[anchor] == nullifier; SBT tokenId uint256(nullifier) owned by anchor

transitions.caller: anyone presenting a valid eID attestation for `anchor`
guards: verifier.verify(anchor, attestation, proof) succeeds (EIP-712, M-of-N signers)

sideEffects.note: mints soulbound tokenId = uint256(nullifier) to anchor

transitions.label: recovery: anchor swap
transitions.caller: anyone presenting a fresh attestation of the SAME eID for `newAnchor`
guards: verifier.verify(newAnchor, ...) yields the person's existing nullifier

sideEffects.note: SBT follows the person to the new anchor (the eID is the recovery key)

transitions.label: liveness refresh (self-loop)
transitions.note: Updates loa and reverifiedAt only; anchor unchanged, no SBT interaction.

transitions.label: governance revocation
transitions.caller: governance (airKUNA DAO Safe) — fraud, death, court order

sideEffects.note: SBT burned; both mappings deleted — the person can re-claim later with a fresh attestation (same nullifier)

notes: NO MONEY FLOWS in this machine — it is a pure identity state machine (money: [] everywhere); the visualizer must render it without a money legend.

UNCLAIMED is both the initial state and the post-revoke state: revoke() deletes the identity entirely, so a revoked person is indistinguishable from a never-claimed one and can re-claim with a fresh attestation (same nullifier).

Governance transitions (setVerifier, setMinLoA, transferGovernance) and verifier signer-set management (addSigner/removeSigner/setThreshold on EIP712Verifier) are configuration, not per-person state — intentionally excluded.
